title: UBI8-INIT: Systemd Fails with Entrypoint script
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I am attempting to setup a container that runs 2 systemd units that work together.  
I am also working in a rootless environment.  
I need to run an entrypoint script to configure the services based on ENV passed into the container.  
I have learned that by using `exec "@"` to launch the CMD it will take on PID 1.  
I have also learned that Podman will [recognize](https://developers.redhat.com/blog/2019/04/24/how-to-run-systemd-in-a-container#enter_podman:~:text=If%20the%20command,without%20any%20modifications.) the CMD as systemd and perform some magic on the backend.  
I am wondering if, because I have an entrypoint, that podman no longer recognizes systemd and does not perform the magic to make it work.  

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
  
Containerfile
```Containerfile
FROM registry.redhat.io/ubi8/ubi-init:8.7-9

COPY --chmod=777 entrypoint.sh /

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["/sbin/init"]
```

Entrypoint.sh
```bash
#!/bin/bash

echo "Starting ${@}"
exec "$@"
```

Build: `podman build -t localhost/playground:latest .`
Run: `podman run -it --rm --replace --name playground localhost/playground:latest`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Starting /sbin/init
Failed to mount tmpfs at /run: Operation not permitted
[!!!!!!] Failed to mount API filesystems, freezing.
Freezing execution.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

My entrypoint script should process correctly and pass on to systemd once it is complete.  
Systemd should then continue to startup and run correctly.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.19.4
Built:        Wed Dec 31 18:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

NOTE: I am using the Alvistack repo to get the latest Podman on Debian.
```
podman/unknown,now 100:4.3.1-1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical Debian GNU/Linux 11 (bullseye)

I am also open to suggestions on better ways to enable runtime configuration on the container when systemd is PID1.

comments.body: You'll need to use `--systemd=always` on the Podman command line we cannot autodetect systemd as init if it's not actually init, but run by an entrypoint script.

Excellent, thank you!
That is what I was missing.

Are you able to share what exactly Podman does on the backend?
I'm probably going to have to document how people can get the container I am developing to work on Docker as well.

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/container_internal_linux.go#L195-L306

Highlights:
- Adds the `container_uuid` environment variable
- Ensures that `/tmp`, `/var/log/journal`, `/run`, and `/run/lock` are on separate tmpfs mounts
- Ensures that cgroups are available inside the container (this is where most of the complexity creeps in, given that we have to worry about cgroups v1 vs v2)
Thank you very much for your help!
